title: Eva 10 in Madrid and Barcelona
wikitext:
[]: Eva 1.0 in Madrid and Barcelona             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 29, 2009 2:53 am see here: http://www.evageeks.org/?p=21

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Jan 29, 2009 5:30 am Wow, this depresses me. I can't think of why they'd be giving 1.0 this attention. Maybe it gives hope for some kind of 'grand welcoming' in the U.S.

[]:             Quote Post by Typhon » Thu Jan 29, 2009 9:04 am This is awsome! I really hope they do something like this over here in the U.S! Hopefully it at least gets more attention than Naruto or Bleach movies (one maybe two days tops at random and remote theatres).

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Jan 29, 2009 2:15 pm Sigh.... And I had just gone through Spain a month ago. Now I'm back Stateside and too busy to travel. OH THE IRONY!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Fri Jan 30, 2009 11:25 am I hope it's not too much to ask it to come to Brazil...

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jan 30, 2009 11:36 am I've seen it already, like 3 times, so I don't care where it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Jan 30, 2009 12:36 pm I wouldn't feel so bad about it. Selecta Visión is known because of their various mishaps with, specially, Evangelion. I'm not much of a dub fan, and consider the Spain sounding castellano dubs quite weird, though it's just a regional thing. But many people considered Selecta's dub of the TV series as the worst dub in castellano. And now for Weva they had to add new cast cuz sum of them already died. I really wouldn't want to hear this new shit, although I read good comments about it. I also read bad comments about this new release's (because the movie in theatres is the first step to a DVD release which is in the works, with betas being sent out and shit) subs... 'tseems they're worst than those fanmade using bablefish.

[]:             Quote Post by Gib » Fri Jan 30, 2009 1:29 pm Selecta Visión wasn't in charge of the Evangelion's dub but Manga Video when VHS still exist.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jan 30, 2009 5:33 pm Action_Bastard wrote: I've seen it already, like 3 times, so I don't care where it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jan 30, 2009 5:39 pm Ornette wrote: Action_Bastard wrote: I've seen it already, like 3 times, so I don't care where it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Fri Jan 30, 2009 7:54 pm Sounds cool, I hope something like this happen with the USA soon.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jan 30, 2009 8:44 pm Aside from the 2 screenings at cons, there's this: http://www.evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=6165

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Feb 03, 2009 8:02 am I read a comment that the guys in Spain didn't use the movie film, but showed the DVD they're working in, but on theaters.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Feb 03, 2009 11:51 am Ewww. Showing a DVD on a film screen completely defeats the purpose of the theatrical experience. If that's true then......EWWWWWWW

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Feb 03, 2009 12:11 pm It would be no surprise from the Land of "Eva First Plane", "Anti Tank Field", "Lyris", "Tablis", the Dokan special misinfo mag, etc...

[]:             Quote Post by AnnieZ » Thu Feb 12, 2009 12:07 pm Madrid and Barcelona KNOW what is good art.

[]:             Quote Post by tian2992 » Thu Mar 19, 2009 9:38 pm AchtungAffen wrote: I wouldn't feel so bad about it. Selecta Visión is known because of their various mishaps with, specially, Evangelion. I'm not much of a dub fan, and consider the Spain sounding castellano dubs quite weird, though it's just a regional thing. But many people considered Selecta's dub of the TV series as the worst dub in castellano. And now for Weva they had to add new cast cuz sum of them already died. I really wouldn't want to hear this new shit, although I read good comments about it. I also read bad comments about this new release's (because the movie in theatres is the first step to a DVD release which is in the works, with betas being sent out and shit) subs... 'tseems they're worst than those fanmade using bablefish.
